distinction_in_major.FB8_1a: To obtain a distinction in a major, a student will be required to obtain first class passes in the courses listed below, except as specified in (b) and (c).

courses_by_major.Applied Mathematics: MAM2046W or (MAM2040F, MAM2041F, MAM2042S and MAM2043S) and MAM3040W or any four of MAM3042F/S, MAM3043F/S, MAM3044F/S, MAM3045F/S and MAM3046F/S
courses_by_major.Artificial Intelligence: CSC2041F, CSC2042S, three of (CSC3041F, CSC3042F, CSC3043S, CSC3044S)
courses_by_major.Biology: BIO2014F, any one of BIO2015F/S, BIO2016S and BIO2017F/S; any two of BIO3013F, BIO3014S, BIO3018F, BIO3019S and BIO3022S
courses_by_major.Business Computing: INF2009F, INF2011S; any two of INF3011F, INF3012S and INF3014F
courses_by_major.Computer Engineering: EEE2049W (or EEE2041F and EEE2042S), EEE2050F, EEE3095S, CSC3022F/CSC3024S
courses_by_major.Environmental & Geographical Science: EGS2013F (or EGS2016F) and EGS2015S, any two of EGS3012S, EGS3020F, EGS3021F, EGS3022S, EGS3023F
courses_by_major.Marine Biology: Any one of BIO2014F, BIO2015F/S, BIO2016S and BIO2017F/S; SEA2004F, BIO3002F, BIO3017S/BIO3022S
courses_by_major.Mathematical Statistics: STA2004F, STA2005S, STA3041F, STA3043S (or STA3047S and STA3048S)
courses_by_major.Mathematics: MAM2000W or (MAM2010F, MAM2011F, MAM2013S and MAM2014S) and MAM3000W or (any four of MAM3010F/S, MAM3011F/S, MAM3012F/S, MAM3014F/S and MAM3015F/S) including at least one of MAM3010F/S and MAM3011F/S
courses_by_major.Quantitative Biology: Any one of BIO2014F, BIO2015F/S, BIO2016S and BIO2017F/S; STA20xxF/S or MAM2046W; BIO3019S; STA30xxF/S or MAM3040W (or MAM3043F/S and any three of MAM3042F/S, MAM3044F/S, MAM3045F/S, MAM3046F/S)
courses_by_major.Statistics & Data Science: STA2004F or STA2030S, one of (STA2005S, STA2007F/S, STA2020F/S), STA3030F or STA3041F, one of (STA3022F, STA3036S, STA3047S and STA3048S)

distinction_in_major.FB8_1b: If a student obtains a first and an upper second class in two 24 credit courses at second-year level, the marks shall be averaged. If this average is 75% or more, the student will be regarded as having first class passes in both courses. The same applies at third-year level with two 36 credit courses.
distinction_in_major.FB8_1c: In special cases the Board of the Faculty may replace a first class in one of the courses listed above by a first class pass in a cognate course (which has not been used for distinction in that cognate subject).

distinction_in_degree.FB8_2a: Obtain a distinction in at least one major (rule FB8.1)
distinction_in_degree.FB8_2b: Obtain first class passes in at least 264 credits, including at least 192 senior credits or obtain an aggregate of at least 75% for each of six 18 credit courses and four 36 credit courses obtained in a minimum period. The minimum period will usually be three years.

note: Only passes at the first attempt (ordinary examinations in June or November and/or deferred examinations) are taken into account. Supplementary examinations are NOT counted.
